narrative: Today's clearest signal across active themes is fire and disturbance ecology: a study of Pinus yunnanensis found individuals in more fire-prone landscapes show stronger serotiny, while a dendrochronological reconstruction linked 19th-century industrialization to a marked pulse in Appalachian fire activity. Climate-linked mortality and regeneration dominated the temperate-forest literature, with a continental analysis of more than 24,000 plots projecting rising background tree mortality under warming, and a Pyrenees study showing canopy cover buffers sapling survival along elevation gradients. Population-level work highlighted conservation stakes for Amazonian jaguars, whose long-term demography revealed a shift toward male-biased floodplain populations, alongside a first global assessment of seasonal variation in avian diets. On the movement front, new three-dimensional step-selection functions extend habitat-selection modelling to species that swim or fly, while a cross-species analysis linked home-range overlap to sociality and territoriality. Conservation science continued its focus on data gaps and recovery, with new hotspot maps of data-deficient vertebrates and a systematic review confirming floodplain restoration benefits for biodiversity, tempered by post-restoration invasive spread. In biogeography, invasive plants in India showed climatic niche shifts tied to residence time, and a new framework combining eDNA, graph theory and niche modelling was proposed for ecosystem assessment. Evolutionary biology offered a genomic look at gene flow in a Central American vector insect shaped by topography, a phylogenomic reassessment of the deep origins of multicellularity in streptophytes, and a conceptual essay drawing lessons for climate adaptation from evolutionary theory.

articles.title: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea individuals are more ready to embrace wildfire in more fire‐prone areas
articles.digest: Fire-prone populations of a resilient Chinese pine show stronger serotiny, revealing local adaptive tuning of a key fire-response trait.

articles.title: Industrialization Drives a Late‐19th‐Century Pulse in Appalachian Fire Activity
articles.digest: Charcoal and tree-ring records show 19th-century industrialization triggered an unusually intense pulse of fire activity across Appalachian forests.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: New benchmarking workflow compares species distribution model performance for marine turtle conservation.

methods_radar.title: A New Framework to Empower Ecosystem Assessment Through the Integration of eDNA Inventories, Graph Theory and Niche Modelling
methods_radar.note: New integrative framework combining eDNA inventories, graph theory and niche modelling for ecosystem assessment.

methods_radar.title: Modelling density surfaces from integrated passive acoustic and point count data streams
methods_radar.note: Novel model integrates passive acoustic and point-count data streams into absolute density surfaces.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: ForestForTrees: new R package inferring forest size-abundance structure from remote sensing.

methods_radar.title: sampley: A Python package for sampling visual survey data
methods_radar.note: sampley: new Python package standardising spatiotemporal sampling of visual survey data.

methods_radar.title: From video to behaviour: An LSTM‐based approach for automated nest behaviour recognition in the wild
methods_radar.note: New LSTM-based deep learning framework for automated nest-behaviour recognition in the wild.

methods_radar.title: Mothbox and Mothbot: Automated light trap and data processing system for scalable insect monitoring
methods_radar.note: Mothbox/Mothbot: new open-source automated light trap plus ML pipeline for insect monitoring.

methods_radar.title: Cell‐based crop phenotyping for future climates
methods_radar.note: Proposes a robotics- and ML-based pathway for high-throughput cell-based crop phenotyping.

methods_radar.title: Ground‐to‐canopy monitoring reveals hidden ecological patterns in Congo Basin mammals
methods_radar.note: Camera-trap monitoring extended vertically (ground-to-canopy) to capture stratified community structure.

methods_radar.title: Spectrally Guided Delineation of Structurally Homogeneous Vegetation Patches Using Sentinel-2 Imagery in Heterogeneous Post-Industrial Landscapes
methods_radar.note: Spectral delineation approach applied to Sentinel-2 imagery for vegetation patch structure mapping.

methods_radar.title: Defaunation Maps for Tropical Mammals and Birds
methods_radar.note: Novel modelling framework links hunting pressure to defaunation, extending beyond remote-sensing approaches.

methods_radar.title: Multiscale species distribution models reveal seascape niche partitioning and overlap in native and alien fishes
methods_radar.note: Multiscale species distribution models extended to seascape niche partitioning in native/alien fishes.

methods_radar.title: A multi-satellite comparison of forest growth between karst and adjacent non-karst nature reserves
methods_radar.note: Multi-satellite dataset comparison benchmarks forest growth monitoring across karst and non-karst reserves.

methods_radar.title: BIMr2: Inference of migration rates and their drivers from multilocus genotypes
methods_radar.note: BIMr2 improves an existing Bayesian method (BIMr) for inferring migration rates from genotypes.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Evaluates and improves reporting of false positive/negative detection errors in eDNA studies.

methods_radar.title: Resolving Emergent Patterns in Community Genetics With Environmental DNA
methods_radar.note: eDNA approach extended to resolve fine-scale community genetic structure alongside composition.

methods_radar.title: Alpine treeline ecotones world wide: fine‐scale spatial patterns and environmental controls
methods_radar.note: Very-high-resolution satellite imagery and landscape metrics applied globally to characterise treeline ecotones.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Dynamic occupancy models applied to track wolf recolonisation patterns in the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Species traits mediate the abundant‐center patterns in ground‐dwelling mammal and bird species in China
methods_radar.note: Trait-mediated analysis applied to test the abundant-center hypothesis in ground-dwelling vertebrates.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Machine-learning algorithms applied to estimate forest carbon density from multi-source data.

methods_radar.title: eDNA sampling reveals no evidence of negative effects of beaver recolonisation on the catchment‐scale distribution of migratory fish
methods_radar.note: Catchment-wide eDNA sampling applied to assess beaver-fish co-distribution impacts.

methods_radar.title: Effects of urban vegetation on the surface urban heat island: a UAV approach with a focus on private gardens
methods_radar.note: UAV thermal imaging applied to assess private-garden cooling effects on the urban heat island.

methods_radar.title: Linking regional and global functional trait data: insights from mammal communities in a fragmented Atlantic Forest landscape
methods_radar.note: Global EltonTraits database evaluated against field-collected functional trait data in fragmented forest.
